value.title: VPN Connection Troubleshooting Guide
value.content:
VPN Troubleshooting

Step 1 - Reinstall AnyConnect
Uninstall via Add/Remove Programs, download the latest from https://itportal/vpn, right-click and Run as Administrator.

Step 2 - Reset network adapters
Device Manager > Network Adapters > right-click every Cisco adapter > Disable, then Enable.

Step 3 - Flush DNS and reset Winsock
Run in an elevated prompt:
netsh int ip reset
netsh winsock reset
ipconfig /flushdns
Restart after each command.

Step 4 - Check credentials
Ensure your AD password is current (https://password.contoso.com). MFA tokens expire after 30 seconds.

If still failing: collect DART logs (AnyConnect Help > Collect Logs) and attach to the ticket.

value.title: Outlook Email Sync Issues
value.content:
Outlook Not Syncing

1. Send/Receive All - Press F9.
2. Work Offline toggle - Send/Receive tab > toggle Work Offline off.
3. Rebuild OST - Close Outlook > rename %localappdata%\Microsoft\Outlook\*.ost to .bak > reopen (takes 10-30 min to sync).
4. New profile - Control Panel > Mail > Show Profiles > Add > set as default.
5. Test connectivity - Hold Ctrl + right-click tray icon > Test Email AutoConfiguration.

Outlook Shows Disconnected: Run Outlook in Safe Mode: outlook.exe /safe. Repair Office via Control Panel > Programs > Office > Change > Quick Repair.

value.title: System Access Request Process
value.content:
Requesting Access

Standard (2 business days)
Submit a ticket with: resource name, access level (Read/Contribute/Owner), business justification, manager email. Your manager receives an approval email. IT provisions via the appropriate AD group.

Privileged access (admin rights, prod systems) - requires CISO approval.

Emergency (<4 hours) - Call ext. 5000 (after hours: +1-800-IT-HELP).

Self-service password reset - https://aka.ms/sspr (requires pre-registered MFA).

Application Access: Manager must submit via ServiceNow with cost center. Emergency Access: Call IT Emergency Line ext. 9999.

value.title: Laptop Hardware Issues Self Service Guide
value.content:
Hardware Troubleshooting

Slow Performance: Restart the laptop, check Task Manager for resource-heavy processes, ensure 10GB free disk space.

Battery not charging - run powercfg /batteryreport. Under 40% design capacity = free warranty replacement (< 3 years old). If battery health under 80%, submit hardware replacement ticket.

Display Issues - Press Win+P to cycle display modes, update display drivers. Broken screen: Accidental damage is covered if you have IT Insurance. Visit Room 204, Building B with your asset tag.

Keyboard/trackpad - Use an external USB keyboard temporarily. Check Device Manager for yellow exclamation marks.

Storage failure - run wmic diskdrive get status. If not OK, raise a Critical ticket immediately. 48-hour replacement SLA.

Loaners - available from Room 204 for up to 5 business days. Bring employee ID. OneDrive must show green tick before handing in your device.

value.title: Network Connectivity Troubleshooting
value.content:
Network Troubleshooting

No Internet Connection: Run ipconfig /release then ipconfig /renew. You should get a 10.x.x.x address. If 169.x.x.x, DHCP is failing. Flush DNS: ipconfig /flushdns. Test with ping 8.8.8.8.

WiFi drops - Forget SSID and reconnect. Prefer CONTOSO-5G (faster, shorter range) over CONTOSO (2.4 GHz). Forget network and reconnect, update WiFi adapter drivers. Connect to CORP-5GHz for better stability. Check status.company.com for outages.

Proxy - Settings > Network > Proxy > http://proxy.contoso.com:8080. Set NO_PROXY for dev: localhost,127.0.0.1,*.contoso.com.

DNS - Primary: 10.0.0.10, Secondary: 10.0.0.11. Test: nslookup contoso.com 10.0.0.10.

value.title: Printer Setup and Printing Problems
value.content:
Printer Troubleshooting

Printer shows Offline
Right-click the printer in Settings > Printers > uncheck 'Use Printer Offline'. If still offline, remove and re-add the printer.

Stuck print queue
Open Services (services.msc) > stop Print Spooler > delete all files in C:\Windows\System32\spool\PRINTERS > start Print Spooler again.

Install a network printer
Go to Settings > Bluetooth & devices > Printers > Add device. If it does not appear automatically, click 'Add manually' and enter the printer IP from the label on the device (e.g. 10.1.2.45). Use the driver from https://itportal/printers matching the model.

Print quality issues
For laser printers: remove the toner cartridge, gently rock it side to side, reinsert. If streaks persist, raise a ticket with the printer asset tag and floor location.

Scanning to email not working
Confirm the device is on the corporate network (not guest WiFi). Check that your email address is registered on the MFD via https://itportal/mfd. SMTP relay: smtp.contoso.com port 25.

value.title: Password Reset and Account Lockout
value.content:
Password Reset and Unlock

Self-service reset (fastest)
Go to https://aka.ms/sspr and follow the prompts. Requires MFA to be pre-registered. Your new password takes effect within 5 minutes across all systems.

Account locked out
After 5 failed attempts your account locks for 30 minutes automatically. Wait and retry, or use SSPR to unlock immediately without waiting.

Password requirements
Minimum 14 characters, at least one uppercase, one number, one symbol. Cannot reuse last 24 passwords. Expires every 180 days; you receive reminder emails at 14, 7 and 1 day before expiry.

Forced reset on next login
If IT resets your password you will be prompted to change it at next login. You cannot reuse the temporary password.

Cannot access SSPR (new device or no MFA registered)
Call the IT Service Desk on ext. 5000 or +1-800-IT-HELP. Identity verification required: employee ID + manager confirmation.

Service account passwords
Must be reset via a Change Request ticket. Coordinate with the application owner to update all dependent services before resetting.

value.title: Microsoft Teams Troubleshooting
value.content:
Microsoft Teams Issues

Teams not loading / blank screen
Quit Teams fully (right-click tray icon > Quit). Delete the cache: %appdata%\Microsoft\Teams (delete all subfolders except 'Downloads'). Relaunch Teams.

Audio problems in meetings
Click the three dots in the meeting > Device settings. Confirm correct speaker and microphone are selected. Test with the audio test call: search 'Make a test call' in Teams settings.

Camera not working
Check Privacy settings: Settings > Privacy > Camera > allow Microsoft Teams. In Device Manager confirm the camera is not disabled. If using an external webcam, try a different USB port.

Screen sharing shows black screen
Go to System Settings > Privacy & Security > Screen Recording > enable for Teams. On Windows, try sharing a specific window instead of the whole desktop if using a multi-monitor setup.

Teams calls drop or are choppy
Run the Microsoft 365 Network Connectivity Test at https://connectivity.office.com. Prefer wired ethernet over WiFi for calls. Set Teams to use a relay server: Teams Settings > Calling > disable 'Direct routing'.

Missing from a Team or Channel
Ask the Team Owner to re-add you. Owners are listed under the Team name > Manage team > Members tab.

value.title: Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) Setup and Issues
value.content:
MFA Setup and Troubleshooting

Set up MFA for the first time
Go to https://aka.ms/mfasetup. Sign in with your corporate account. Click 'Add method' and choose Microsoft Authenticator (recommended) or phone number. Follow the QR code scan steps in the Authenticator app.

Got a new phone
Do not delete the old authenticator before setting up the new device. Go to https://aka.ms/mfasetup > add the new phone as a second method > verify it works > then remove the old phone entry.

MFA prompt not arriving
Check signal and data connection. In the Authenticator app tap 'Refresh'. If using SMS, ensure the registered number is correct at https://aka.ms/mfasetup. Alternatively approve via the app notification rather than SMS.

Locked out with no MFA access (e.g. lost phone)
Call IT Service Desk on ext. 5000 with your employee ID and manager's name. IT can issue a one-time bypass code (valid 10 minutes). You must re-register MFA immediately after logging in.

MFA fatigue / unexpected prompts
If you receive MFA requests you did not initiate, deny them immediately and call the Security team on ext. 5911. This may indicate a credential compromise.

value.title: OneDrive and SharePoint Sync Issues
value.content:
OneDrive / SharePoint Sync

OneDrive stuck syncing or shows errors
Click the OneDrive tray icon > Settings > Account > Unlink this PC. Sign back in with your corporate account. OneDrive will re-sync (existing local files are kept).

Files show as red X
Right-click the file > OneDrive > Always keep on this device. If path length exceeds 260 characters, shorten the folder names or move the file to a shorter path. OneDrive does not support file names containing: \ / : * ? " < > |

SharePoint library not syncing
In SharePoint online, open the library > Sync button. Each library must be synced individually. Maximum 300,000 files per library is supported.

Storage quota exceeded
Check your usage at https://portal.office.com/account > Storage. Archive old files to the Departmental Archive SharePoint site. Request a quota increase via IT portal (manager approval needed for > 1 TB).

Known Folder Move (Desktop/Documents/Pictures backup)
Ensure Known Folder Move is enabled: OneDrive settings > Backup > Manage backup. This protects your files if your laptop is replaced or reimaged.

Access denied on a SharePoint site
Request access via the 'Request access' link on the site, or ask the Site Owner to grant you permission. Site Owners are listed under Site Settings > Site permissions.

value.title: Software Installation and Licence Requests
value.content:
Software Installation

Installing approved software
All approved software is available in the IT Self-Service Portal at https://itportal/software. Search for the application and click Install. No admin rights required. Installation typically completes within 15 minutes.

Requesting software not in the portal
Raise a Software Request ticket with: application name, version, vendor website, business justification, cost estimate, manager approval. Security review takes 5 business days. Legal/procurement review required for paid software.

Admin rights for installation
Admin rights are not granted to end users by default. Submit a Temporary Admin Rights request for a 4-hour window. An IT engineer will shadow the session remotely.

Office / Microsoft 365 apps
Available via https://portal.office.com > Install apps. You can install on up to 5 devices. If activation fails, sign out of all Office apps, run the Office Sign-in Reset tool from the portal, and sign back in.

Software crashes or won't launch
Run a Quick Repair first: Control Panel > Programs > right-click the app > Repair. If that fails, uninstall and reinstall from the portal. Check Windows Event Viewer > Application log for error codes to include in your ticket.

value.title: Windows Update and Patch Management
value.content:
Windows Update

Updates not installing
Open Settings > Windows Update > Check for updates. If updates download but fail to install, run: sfc /scannow in an elevated prompt, then DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /RestoreHealth. Restart and try again.

Computer stuck on 'Installing updates' at shutdown
Wait at least 30 minutes before force-powering off. If still stuck, hold the power button for 10 seconds, power on, and Windows will roll back the failed update automatically.

Update caused a problem (blue screen / app broken)
Boot into Safe Mode (hold Shift + Restart > Troubleshoot > Startup Settings > Safe Mode). Go to Settings > Windows Update > View update history > Uninstall updates. Remove the most recently installed update.

Managed update schedule
Corporate laptops receive patches via WSUS on the second Tuesday of each month (Patch Tuesday + 7 days). You will receive a notification with a 72-hour deferral window. Do not defer Critical security patches.

Force a policy refresh
If your machine has not received updates for over 30 days, run: gpupdate /force in an elevated prompt, then wuauclt /detectnow. Contact IT if updates are still not appearing after 24 hours.

value.title: Remote Desktop and VDI Access
value.content:
Remote Desktop Access

Connecting via Remote Desktop (RDP)
Open Remote Desktop Connection (mstsc.exe). Enter the hostname or IP of the target machine (must be on corporate network or VPN). Use CONTOSO\yourusername as the username. The target machine must have 'Allow remote connections' enabled and you must be in the Remote Desktop Users group.

RDP connection refused
Confirm VPN is connected first. Check that the target machine is powered on (IT portal > My Devices shows last seen time). Firewall rule for RDP (TCP 3389) must be open - raise a firewall request ticket if needed.

Azure Virtual Desktop (AVD)
Access via https://aka.ms/avd or install the Windows App from the Microsoft Store. Sign in with your corporate account. Published desktops and apps are assigned by your manager via an access request.

Citrix session disconnects frequently
In Citrix Workspace app, go to Advanced Settings > Session reliability and increase the reconnect time to 180 seconds. Prefer a wired connection. Citrix HDX protocol requires at least 2 Mbps stable bandwidth.

Cannot print from remote session
Ensure 'Printers' is checked under Local Resources in the RDP client before connecting. For AVD, install the Universal Print driver from the IT portal on your local machine.

value.title: Antivirus, Malware and Security Incidents
value.content:
Security Incidents and Antivirus

Suspected malware or ransomware
IMMEDIATELY disconnect the machine from the network (unplug ethernet and disable WiFi). Do not power off. Call the Security team on ext. 5911 (24/7). Do not attempt to clean the infection yourself. Preserve all files and logs.

Phishing email received
Do not click any links or open attachments. Use the 'Report Phishing' button in Outlook (Home ribbon) to forward to the security team automatically. Delete the email from your inbox and Deleted Items.

Antivirus alert / quarantine
Microsoft Defender quarantine notifications are normal when a threat is blocked. Go to Windows Security > Protection history to review. If a legitimate file is quarantined, raise a ticket with the file path and application name for whitelisting.

Defender is disabled or showing red
Open Windows Security (search in Start menu). Click each protection area and turn it on. If Defender is greyed out, it may be managed by group policy. Run: Get-MpComputerStatus in PowerShell and include the output in your ticket.

Suspicious account activity (unexpected logins or MFA prompts)
Change your password immediately at https://aka.ms/sspr. Call the Security team on ext. 5911. Review your recent sign-in activity at https://mysignins.microsoft.com. Do not wait - account compromises escalate quickly.

value.title: New Employee IT Onboarding Checklist
value.content:
New Employee IT Setup

Before day one (completed by your manager)
Submit a New Starter request in ServiceNow at least 5 business days before the start date. Include: full name, job title, department, cost centre, start date, required software, and whether a laptop or desktop is needed.

Day one — what IT will have ready
- Corporate laptop, pre-imaged with Windows 11 and standard software
- Active Directory account and email address (firstname.lastname@contoso.com)
- Microsoft 365 licence assigned
- Badge access to the building and your floor

Day one steps for the new employee
1. Collect equipment from Room 204, Building B (bring offer letter or employee ID).
2. Power on the laptop and complete the Windows Hello setup (PIN + fingerprint recommended).
3. Sign in to Microsoft 365 at https://portal.office.com and register MFA at https://aka.ms/mfasetup.
4. Connect to CONTOSO-CORP WiFi using your corporate credentials.
5. Open the IT Self-Service Portal at https://itportal and install any additional software you need.

First week
- Check with your manager for system-specific access requests (shared mailboxes, SharePoint sites, business applications).
- Complete the mandatory Cyber Security Awareness training in the Learning Portal within 5 business days.

IT contacts
Service Desk: ext. 5000 | it-help@contoso.com | https://itportal
